metadata.source: Meridian artifacts (MRL, Control List, CSC-01, CSP SOC 2 report)

controls.title: User Entity Workspace - IP Whitelisting
controls.descriptionText: As part of user entity onboarding, the Engineering team restricts access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configurations within Quanta Assist backend infrastructure to determine whether the Engineering team had restricted access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).

Inspected the configurations within Quanta Assist backend infrastructure to determine whether the Engineering team had restricted access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of client using Quanta Assist product hosted on GCP, screenshot showing whitelisting of client IPs.

controls.title: Access Review - GCP
controls.descriptionText: The CloudOps team performs a review of the administrative access to GCP, Okta, MongoDB, Datadog, PagerDuty, DevRev, and GitHub on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of quarter, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team performed a review of the administrative access to GCP, Okta, MongoDB, Datadog, PagerDuty, DevRev, and GitHub and whether the CISO provided sign off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For the latest quarter, complete access review tickets for GCP for Quanta Assist product.

controls.title: Single sign on - Cloud and supporting tools
controls.descriptionText: Access to GCP, GitHub, PagerDuty, DevRev and Datadog is restricted using single sign on (SSO) enforced by Okta.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configuration within Okta to determine whether access to GCP, GitHub, PagerDuty, DevRev and Datadog is restricted through Okta SSO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to GCP is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to Github is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to PagerDuty is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to DevRev is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to Datadog is restricted through Okta

controls.title: Privilege Access Management - GCP
controls.descriptionText: Privileged access to GCP resources hosting Quanta Assist infrastructure is restricted and governed through GCP Identity Management Policies.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations to determine whether privileged access to GCP resources hosting Quanta Assist infrastructure was restricted and governed through GCP Identity Management Policies.
evidenceRequirementTexts: GCP configuration screenshots showing that privileged access to Quanta Assist‑hosting GCP resources is limited to authorized roles via Identity Management policies.

controls.title: Backup Configuration - GCP - Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: GCP Backup is used by the Meridian for backup of GCP services. Full backup of GCP SQL (MySQL and MongoDB) is scheduled every 24 hours, and retention is set to 7 day. Alerts are configured on GCP Backup and DR Service to log into GCP Monitoring.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration on GCP Backup to determine whether full backup of GCP SQL (MySQL and MongoDB) was scheduled every 24 hours and retention was set to 7 day.

Additionally, inspected the configuration of GCP Backup to determine whether alerts were configured to be logged into GCP Monitoring
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing the backup schedule for Quanta Assist hosted on GCP.
Screenshot from the retention period of backup for Quanta Assist hosted on GCP.
GCP Backup configuration screenshots showing that backup‑related alerts are logged and monitored.

controls.title: Backup Restoration Tests
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs backup restoration and recovery tests of Quanta Assist databases hosted on Google Cloud Platform on a quarterly basis. Results of test are recorded within a Jira ticket. Failures in restoration, if any, are fixed by taking appropriate corrective actions and recording / documenting the same within the Jira ticket. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within the Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of quarter, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps Team performed backup restoration and recovery tests of backed up Quanta Assist databases hosted on Google Cloud Platform and whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For the latest quarter, JIRA ticket showing that backup restoration tests were performed.
For the latest quarter, JIRA ticket showing that Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery (DR) Drill
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Meridian conducts disaster recovery simulation / tabletop exercise for the Quanta Assist product hosted on Google Cloud Platform and its supporting infrastructure to test its business continuity and disaster recovery measures and determine the effectiveness of mitigating controls for business disruption risks. Post the activity, DR drill report is prepared by the CloudOps team, highlighting the tests conducted, test results, and improvement areas. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within a Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the DR drill report for Quanta Assist to determine whether a disaster recovery simulation / tabletop exercise was conducted and whether the drill documented controls and test results for infrastructure availability, system and data availability, uninterrupted process flow and training of personnel to handle disaster.

Additionally, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DR drill report for Quanta Assist product hosted on Google Cloud Platform
JIRA ticket showing the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.

controls.title: Data Purging
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled configurations to automatically purge (permanently delete) data within GCP based on expiry of lifecycle rules.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within GCP administrative console to determine whether data within GCP was automatically purged based on expiry of lifecycle rules.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing Lifecycle rules within GCP showing the schedule as to when data is set to expire product hosted on Google Cloud Platform
Screenshot showing that data was set to be permanently deleted (purged) upon expiry within Quanta Assist product hosted on Google Cloud Platform

controls.title: Data Flow Diagram
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a data flow diagram for Quanta Assist to identify and track the flow of data between internal components and external entities, including data sources, transmission paths, and system boundaries.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the data flow diagram to determine whether the flow of data between internal components and external entities, including data sources, transmission paths, and system boundaries were tracked.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data flow diagram for Quanta Assist product hosted on Google Cloud Platform

controls.title: Web Application Firewall
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled WAF between Quanta Assist product and the internet to monitor, filter, and block HTTPS traffic as well as any potential attacks such as distributed denial of service (DDoS) on its web application.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within GCP administrative console to determine whether GCP WAF was enabled between Quanta Assist and the internet to monitor, filter, and block HTTPS traffic as well as any potential attacks on its web application.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing GCP WAF was enabled between Quanta Assist products and the internet.

controls.title: Security Groups - GCP
controls.descriptionText: Access to the GCP cloud environment hosting the Quanta Assist product and supporting infrastructure is restricted by the respective CloudOps team using GCP Firewall.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected GCP administrative console to determine whether GCP Firewall was configured to restrict access to GCP cloud environment hosting the Quanta Assist product and supporting infrastructure to the CloudOps team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from GCP administrative console showing GCP Firewall was configured to restrict access to GCP cloud environment hosting the Quanta Assist product.

controls.title: GCP Security Groups - ruleset review
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs GCP Security Groups ruleset review on an annual basis by extracting the GCP Security Groups ruleset from the GCP platform and reviewing them with the help of Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team to identify stale / inaccurate / missing rules. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the GCP Security ruleset review Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team performed a review of GCP Security Groups ruleset and whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: JIRA ticket showing annual GCP Security Groups ruleset review was performed
JIRA ticket showing Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign off.

controls.title: Encryption - GCP
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled default encryption provided within GCP to encrypt data-at-rest using AES 256-bit encryption method.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the administrative console of GCP to determine whether Quanta Assist application data, including backup copies, were encrypted at rest.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing that encryption is enable within GCP to encrypt data-at-rest using AES 256-bit encryption menthod.

controls.title: Encryption - Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured Quanta Assist to transmit application data over the internet using Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) over TLS v1.2 / TLS v1.3.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected Quanta Assist product certificate to determine whether Quanta Assist product were configured to transmit application data over the internet using Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) over TLS v1.2 or v1.3.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing that Quanta Assist web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - GCP Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: GCP CloudTrail is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within GCP Services using alerts. Alerts are configured by the CloudOps team to be re-directed to Datadog. Alerts that cross pre-defined thresholds have been configured to be sent as messages to Slack. The CloudOps team generates a JIRA ticket for confirmed true positive cases and tracks it to closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether alerts generated by GCP CloudTrail was configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether alerts that cross pre-defined thresholds were configured to be sent as messages to Slack.

Further, for a sample of JIRA tickets related to logging and monitoring of GCP Infrastructure, inspected the ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Datadog showing that GCP logs such as GCP Cost Coverage, GCP RDS ACU Utilization, etc. are being ingested by Datadog.
Screenshot from Datadog showing that alerts are sent to Meridian Slack based on pre-defined thresholds
System generated list of alerts from Datadog for the audit period.
For a sample of alerts, Jira tickets that are raised.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: Luigi is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within Quanta Assist using alerts. Alerts are configured by the Quanta Assist Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is configured to send a Slack message and raise a PagerDuty incident when thresholds are exceeded. The Quanta Assist Engineering team analyses these PagerDuty incidents and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within PagerDuty until closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Luigi to determine whether alerts generated by Quanta Assist were configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether it was configured to automatically send a Slack message and raise PagerDuty incidents when thresholds were exceeded.

Further, for a sample of PagerDuty incidents related to logging and monitoring of Quanta Assist, inspected the ticket to determine whether the Quanta Assist Engineering team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Luigi Task Status page showcasing that Luigi is used for monitoring for Quanta Assist and Quanta Assist logs
Screenshot from alert manager within Luigi showcasing that Datadog was configured to receive Luigi alerts
Screenshot showing that alerts are sent to Meridian Slack and PagerDuty based on pre-defined thresholds
System generated list of incidents from PagerDuty specific to logging and monitoring of Quanta Assist for the audit period.
For a sample of incidents, PagerDuty tickets showing that the incidents were analyzed and tracked to closure.

controls.title: Capacity management - GCP
controls.descriptionText: Karpenter is utilized to maintain Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist application availability and optimize cluster efficiency by dynamically autoscaling GCP Kubernetes pods and EC2 instances based on workload demands.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Karpenter terraform configurations defined for Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist to determine whether Karpenter was utilized to maintain Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist application availability and optimize cluster efficiency by dynamically autoscaling GCP Kubernetes pods and EC2 instances based on workload demands.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Terraform configuration files showing that Karpenter is configured for X‑Platform and U‑Portfolio products to dynamically autoscale Kubernetes pods and EC2 instances in order to maintain application availability and optimize cluster efficiency.

controls.title: Reporting to User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has developed a status webpage (https://status.actioniq.com) that offers visibility into the Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist’s response and availability metrics across all cloud service providers and geographical instances. User entities can opt to subscribe to be notified of system incidents within this webpage.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the status webpage for Quanta Assist to determine whether application response and availability metrics across all cloud service providers and geographical instances were reported to the user entities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Quanta Assist's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Quanta Self-Serve's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Quanta Analyze's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Quanta Discover's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Zephyr Console's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Quanta Assist's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Quanta Capture NG's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.

controls.title: Support procedures for User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has prepared and maintained a support website (https://help.actioniq.com/) containing resources such as product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums to help user entities effectively utilize Quanta Assist.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the support website for Quanta Assist to determine whether resources such as product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums were documented.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Quanta Assist's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums
Screenshot from Quanta Self-Serve's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums
Screenshot from Quanta Analyze's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums
Screenshot from Quanta Discover's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.
Screenshot from Zephyr Console's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums
Screenshot from Quanta Assist's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums
Screenshot from Quanta Capture NG's web page showing the support page documenting product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums

controls.title: User Entity Onboarding
controls.descriptionText: During user entity onboarding, the Professional Services team informs the Engineering and CloudOps teams to provide access to the user entity through a Jira Ticket. Administrator privileges for the user entity account are provided only to personnel explicitly identified and authorized by the user entity. Once access is provisioned, ownership and management of the account are formally handed over to the user entity prior to closing the Jira ticket.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of user entities, inspected the JIRA ticket to determine whether a unique tenancy with administrator accounts to the personnel identified from the user entity organization was provisioned by the CloudOps team based on the details provided in the JIRA ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of customers onboarded during the audit period using all Quanta Assist.

For a sample of customers, JIRA ticket/email communication showing a unique tenancy with administrator accounts to the personnel identified from the user entity organization was provisioned by the CloudOps team based on the details provided.
For a sample of customers, JIRA ticket/email communication showing that the customers were informed once they were onboarded.

controls.title: User Entity Offboarding
controls.descriptionText: Upon repatriation of a user entity, the Professional Services team informs the Customer Support team, based on which a Jira ticket tagging the Engineering and CloudOps team is raised. The GCP S3, GCP RDS, GCP Redshift, and MongoDB Databases of the repatriating user entity are deleted, and relevant details are documented within the Jira ticket. Data stored within the backup files are deleted in accordance with their respective backup retention configuration.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of repatriated user entities within the Quanta Assist, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team deleted the GCP S3, GCP RDS, GCP Redshift, and MongoDB databases in accordance with the Meridian Tenant Deletion Policy.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of offboarded customers, JIRA ticket showing that CloudOps team deleted the GCP S3, GCP RDS, GCP Redshift, and MongoDB databases.

controls.title: Customer Support Workflow
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a customer support workflow to identify and track the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to the Quanta Assist.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the customer support workflow to determine whether the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to the  Quanta Assist were tracked.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Documentation showing that Meridian has customer support workflow documented to identify and track the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to Quanta Assist.

controls.title: Customer Support Tickets
controls.descriptionText: User entities can report issues by raising an issue from the front end of Quanta Assist, sending an email to “XYZ@meridian.example”, or raise a ticket via “https://help.XYZ.com”. These are recorded as customer support tickets within the DevRev Ticketing Tool. Agents within the Customer Support team analyses the customer query and provides resolution by coordinating with other teams within Meridian, as required. Upon resolution, the DevRev ticket is closed.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist incidents raised by user entities, inspected the DevRev ticket to determine whether resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets showing resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Data Ingestion, Processing, and Export
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured Luigi to monitor Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist platform for data ingestion, data duplication, data processing, and / or data export errors through alerts. Alerts are configured by the Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is monitored by the Customer Support team. The Customer Support team analyses these Datadog alerts and raises DevRev tickets for true positive cases and documents the details of analysis, root cause, and prevention and mitigation steps within the DevRev ticket until closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Luigi to determine whether alerts generated by Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist platform was configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, for a sample of DevRev tickets related to data ingestion, data duplication, data processing, and / or data export errors, inspected the ticket to determine whether the Customer Support team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Luigi Task Status page showcasing that Luigi is used for monitoring for Quanta Assist and Quanta Assist logs
Screenshot from alert manager within Luigi showcasing that Datadog was configured to receive Luigi alerts
For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets showing resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Data Ingestion Reports
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured a row level reconciliation process to execute before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data. Results of reconciliation are documented within a DevRev ticket.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of DevRev tickets related to data ingestion, inspected the ticket to determine whether a row level reconciliation process was executed before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets related to data ingestion showing that a row level reconciliation process was executed before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data.

controls.title: New Hire Induction
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to sign the following documents at the time of joining to acknowledge the acceptance of terms to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of information within the organization:
1. Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC), and
2. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA).
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the Appointment Letter and Non Disclosure Agreement (NDA) acknowledgement to determine whether the users had signed and agreed to preserve ethical values and the confidentiality and integrity of information within Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners, Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC)
For a sample of new joiners, Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)

controls.title: Employee Handbook
controls.descriptionText: Formalized employee handbook is documented to communicate expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures. This document is published on U-Central and accessible by all users.
controls.testProcedure:
Performed inquiry with Senior HR Operations Specialist to determine whether formalized employee handbook was published on U-Central and accessible by all users.

Additionally, inspected the employee handbook to determine whether it communicated expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from U-Central showing that employee handbook was published on U-Central and is accessible by all users.
Employee Handbook

controls.title: Background and Reference Checks
controls.descriptionText:
The Human Resource team is responsible for carrying out background checks for new hires before the date of hire with the help of a third-party vendor, who conducts these checks and provides a background check report to Meridian. The following background checks are performed:
► International Criminal Searches,
► International Global Watchlist,
► International Education Verification, and
► International Employment Verification.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the background verification reports to determine whether background check was performed before the date of hire for parameters such as International Criminal Searches, International Global Watchlist, International Education Verification, and International Employment Verification.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners, BGV reports.

controls.title: Technical Interviews
controls.descriptionText: New hires are required to undergo technical interviews prior to appointment to help ensure that skill sets match with job requirements.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the technical interview assessment forms to determine whether the skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners for Bangalore & Chennai locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for London and Valencia locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for Palo Alto and New York locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

controls.title: User Trainings - New Hires
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to attend the following virtual trainings on Mindtickle platform during induction containing elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches:
1. Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct, and
2. Information Security and Compliance.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of new hires, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the new hires were made aware of code of conduct and information security and compliance during induction.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material
For a sample of new joiners, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: User Trainings - Active Users
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, all active users are required to attend the following virtual Information Security and Compliance refresher trainings on Mindtickle platform, which includes information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the refresher Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of active users, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the active users completed the refresher trainings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Refresher Information Security and Compliance training material for active users
For a sample of active users, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: Performance Evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Performance evaluations are carried out on a bi-annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six month. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of active employees who have been in Meridian for more than six month, inspected the performance evaluation results within Workday to determine whether performance evaluations were carried out by employees and their reporting managers.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, performance evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: VCSOM evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Vision, Culture, Strategy, Operating Model (VCSOM) evaluations are carried out on an annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six month. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out VCSOM evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of active employees who have been in Meridian for more than six month, inspected the VCSOM evaluation results within Workday to determine whether VCSOM evaluations were carried out by employees and their reporting managers.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, VCSOM evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: Organization Chart
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has established an organizational structure to define reporting lines, authorities, and responsibilities for NXSC to meet its objectives for providing Software as a Service (SaaS) services to its user entities.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Meridian organizational chart to determine whether Meridian has defined an organizational structure which describes the line of authority and the individual roles and responsibilities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian's organizational chart

controls.title: User job roles and responsibilities
controls.descriptionText: Roles and responsibilities are defined in written job descriptions to assign responsibility for security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity.
controls.testProcedure:
Inquired the Information Security Senior Manager to determine whether the Job Descriptions were updated within Workday and reflect the current job responsibilities.

Additionally, for a sample of Job Descriptions, inspected the job description to determine whether roles and responsibilities were defined.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of Job Descriptions, job description document showing that roles and responsibilities were defined.

controls.title: Risk Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Risk Management Procedure that contains guidelines for the identification, preparation, and categorization of risks to satisfy business information, legal and regulatory requirements. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Risk Management Procedure to determine whether guidelines for the identification, preparation, and categorization of risks to satisfy business information, legal and regulatory requirements were defined and documented.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central and was reviewed and approved by CISO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Risk Management Procedure document
Screenshot from U-Central showing that Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Annual Risk Assessment
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs risk assessment of the various functions within Meridian with the support of internal Meridian stakeholders and prepares a risk register. Risks are identified based on inherent risk value, internal and external threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and potential impact to Meridian’s objectives, assets, and operations. Risks are evaluated based on impact magnitude and expectancy criteria in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability. Mitigating controls are identified to bring the inherent risk to acceptable levels and is documented within a Jira ticket.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Risk Register and corresponding Jira ticket(s) to determine whether the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest Risk Register

Jira ticket(s) showing the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Onboarding Assessments
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance Team performs vendor onboarding assessments for new vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. The Information Security & Compliance team requests independent compliance and security related certifications to assess whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of vendors and third party service providers onboarded during the audit period, inspected the onboarding assessment report to determine whether Information Security & Compliance team assessed whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, vendor onboarding assessment reports

controls.title: Management Review Meetings (MRM)
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Management Review Meetings (MRM) are conducted amongst the Executive Management, Steering Committee (SSC), CISO, Legal team, Privacy team, and the  Information Security & Compliance teams to ensure security initiatives are in line with company objectives and to discuss non-conformities and findings identified during information security internal audits / risk assessments / any other assessments, incidents, and their respective corrective actions were performed.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Management Review Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes to determine whether the Executive Management, Steering Committee (SSC), CISO, Legal team, Privacy team, and the  Information Security & Compliance teams met annually to ensure security initiatives were in line with company objectives and discussed nonconformities and findings identified during information security internal audits / risk assessments / other assessments, incidents, and their respective corrective actions.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Management Review Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Board of Directors Independence
controls.descriptionText: Meridian operates under the direction of its Board of Directors. The Board of Directors comprises of members independent from Meridian’s daily operations.
controls.testProcedure:
Performed inquiry with Meridian CISO to determine whether Board of Directors constituted of members independent of Meridian daily operations.

Additionally, inspected the ‘List of Directors’ and Board of Directors structure of Meridian to determine whether the Board of Directors demonstrated independence from Meridian's daily operations.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of Board of Directors

controls.title: Board of Directors Meeting
controls.descriptionText: On a quarterly basis, matters related to Meridian’s results of operations, cybersecurity updates, projections for the coming quarter, and any major changes, are discussed between the Board of Directors and key members of management.
controls.testProcedure: For sample of quarter inspected the Board of Directors Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes to determine whether matters related to Meridian’s results of operations, projections for the coming quarter, and any major changes were discussed between Board of Directors and key members of management.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarter, Board of Directors Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Information Security Internal Audit
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, an information security management system related internal audit is conducted to assess the operating effectiveness of controls related to information security and information technology. This audit is conducted for Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist, User Access Management, Information Security, Network Security, Endpoint Security, Physical Security, and Human Resource Security. Observations or issues identified during the audit are documented in the internal audit report along with recommendations for resolutions of the observations or issues and a management response is sought for action plans.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Internal Audit Report to determine whether information security and information technology related internal audit was conducted to assess the operating effectiveness of controls related to information security and information technology for Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist, User Access Management, Information Security, Data Privacy, Network Security, Endpoint Security, Physical Security, and Human Resource Security.

Additionally, inspected the relevant Jira tickets to determine whether observations were tracked to closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Internal Audit Report for the audit period
Email communication showing that internal audit report is shared with the respective stakeholders.

controls.title: Subservice Organization Assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance team assesses the subservice organization’s compliance with the applicable security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity commitments; and evaluates the impact of any noted exceptions of relevant controls and Complementary User Entity Controls by obtaining and evaluating their annual System and Organization Control attestation report.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Subservice Organization Assessment report to determine whether the GRC team had assessed the subservice organizations’ compliance with security, confidentiality, availability, processing integrity, and privacy commitments by evaluating their System and Organization Control attestation report.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Subservice Organization Assessment report for GCP
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Freshworks
Subservice Organization Assessment report for ReliaQuest
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Microsoft Speech Services
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Duplo Cloud

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Annual Re-assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance Team performs a re-assessment for all vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. Observations identified during the assessment are communicated to the third-party service providers and are tracked to closure by the Information Security & Compliance team.
controls.testProcedure:
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, inspected the re-assessment report to determine whether Information Security & Compliance team assessed whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.

Additionally, inspected the relevant Jira tickets to determine whether observations were tracked to closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of all active vendors and third party service providers as on date from Streamline
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, re-assessment report
Jira tickets showing observations were tracked to closure.

controls.title: ISO 27001
controls.descriptionText:
2022 and PCI DSS v4.0:

controls.title: Global Information Security Policy
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has defined and developed the Global Information Security Policy addressing matters related to system security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity principles.

Additionally, the following are also defined:
► Meridian's objectives pertaining to integrity and ethical values
 i. Code of Business Conduct
 ii. Role Competencies
 iii. Disciplinary Policies
► Human Resources security policy for hiring, termination, and periodic performance appraisals of users,
► Clear desk and clear screen policy,
► Media Handling Guidelines for electronic and paper based media disposal,
► Access Control Policies for user access management, privilege management, and password management, and
► Physical Security Policy for physical security and environmental safeguards.

It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Global Information Security Policy to determine whether system security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity principles; code of business conduct, role competencies, disciplinary policies, human resources security policy, clear desk and clear screen policy, media handling guidelines, access control policies, and physical security policies were defined and documented.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Global Information Security Policy was published on U-Central and was reviewed and approved annually by CISO.

Screenshot from U-Central showing Global Information Security Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Access Creation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: When a user joins the organization, HR team creates a user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically sends an email communication to the IT team and Workspace Experience team and creates an account within the local Azure Active Directory.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory to determine whether an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically created when a user ID is created within Workday HRMS tool.

Additionally, for a sample of new joiners, inspected the automated Workday emails and access creation logs within Azure Active Directory to determine whether the local Azure Active Directory accounts were created.

controls.title: Access Revocation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: On the last working day of a user, whether resignation, termination, or absconding, HR team deactivates the user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically deactivates the user's account within the local Azure Active Directory. The IT team validates the access revocation of the user on the last working day and confirms the same via email.

Inspected the configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory to determine whether an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically deactivated when a user ID is deactivated within Workday HRMS tool.

Additionally, for a sample of leavers, inspected the access revocation logs within Azure Active Directory to determine whether the user's access was revoked on or before their last working date.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory that an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically deactivated when a user ID is deactivated within Workday HRMS tool.
Screenshot showing configuration within Workday that the global offboarding email is sent automatically to the IT team on the last working date of the employee.

For a sample of leavers, email communication showing that IT team confirmed that the access to Azure AD account is deactivated.

controls.title: Access Review - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs a review of the administrative access to firewalls and Okta on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the respective team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.

evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarter, complete access review JIRA tickets for firewall and Okta.

controls.title: Password and Account Lockout Policy – Active Directory
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented Azure Active Directory to enforce password and account lockout policy settings on end user workstations. These policy settings include password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 day. Multi-factor authentication is enforced for all users added to the Azure Active Directory. User accounts are locked after 5 invalid logon attempts and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the group policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory to determine whether password settings included configurations to enforce password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 day, account lockout after 5 invalid logon attempts, and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of Password policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory

controls.title: Idle Session Timeout - Active Directory
controls.descriptionText: Idle session timeout settings are controlled through group policy settings on the Azure Active Directory and is configured to terminate unattended end user workstations within 10 [India] / 30 [US] minutes of inactivity with password protected screensaver.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the group policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory to determine whether unattended end user workstations were terminated within 10 [India] / 30 [US] minutes of inactivity with password protected screensaver.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of group policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory showing that unattended end user workstations are terminated within 10 [India] / 30 [US] minutes of inactivity with password protected screensaver.

controls.title: Firewall Rules
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has implemented perimeter firewalls on the Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia delivery center internet gateways to proactively identify and protect Meridian network against security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity threats from sources outside the system boundaries. Rules are enforced on the firewalls to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the management approved network diagrams of Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia to determine whether firewall was implemented on the delivery center's internet gateway.

Additionally, inspected the configurations within the firewalls to determine whether rules were configured and enforced to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Management approved network diagrams for all in-scope locations
Screenshot showing 'deny all' rule is configured within firewall for all in-scope locations
Screenshot showing protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks are configured within firewall for all in-scope locations
Screenshot showing  Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters is configured within firewall for all in-scope locations

controls.title: Coding - Peer Review
controls.descriptionText: A developer performs coding in the development environment. A peer or Technical Lead carries out a code review to ensure completeness and accuracy of the code and results of the review are documented within GitHub. Upon successful completion of the code review, the peer reviewer provides sign off within GitHub, and the developer initiates security testing.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of commits on the GitHub master branch, inspected the signoff within GitHub to determine whether a peer (an employee other than the developer of the code) had conducted a code review to ensure completeness and accuracy of the code prior to initiating security testing.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Configurations of the branch protection rule showing that peer review can not be bypassed for Quanta Assist (hosted on GCP).
For a sample of commits, screenshot of signoff within GitHub showing an employee other than the developer of the code had conducted a code review.

controls.title: Security Testing
controls.descriptionText: Upon successful completion of the peer code review, a scheduled job is automatically run to perform Snyk static code analysis. Deployment of code onto production is restricted until all security checks have passed.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of commits on the GitHub master branch,, inspected the status of all-checks-pass.yml job [or] Snyk static code analysis job within GitHub to determine whether all security checks had passed prior to deployment of code onto production.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of commits, screenshotf of the status of 'all-checks-pass.yml' or 'Synk static code analysis' job within Github.

controls.title: Unit and Functional Testing - Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: QA Team and developers define the test cases applicable for Unit and Functional testing at the beginning of the development cycle. These test cases are either configured in the workflow files within the respective GitHub repositories and are configured to ensure that they are executed successfully before the source code is packaged into a build. Source code compilation runs in parallel with testing, and builds are generated only after successful test execution and SonarQube code‑coverage checks.
controls.testProcedure:
For a sample of commits on the GitHub master branch for Quanta Assist, inspected the inspected the GitHub workflow to determine whether the pipelines contained work-steps configured for Unit and Integration Testing, and whether the work-steps were completed successfully.

For a sample of commits on the GitHub master branch for Quanta Assist, inspected the GitHub pipelines to determine whether the SonarQube work step for code coverage checks was completed successfully.

Screenshot of conditions and branch protection rules in the code repository that tests must pass before proceeding to build/package steps.
For a sample of commits, screenshots from GitHub showing CI and CD automation sprints which show that various steps of the workflow (pipeline checks) were executed successfully for Unit Testing.
Screenshot from YAML or pipeline configurations showing the steps that trigger build jobs.
Screenshot from YAML or pipeline configurations showing the steps for unit and functional tests within the same workflow.

controls.title: Source-Code Reviews
controls.descriptionText: Meridian conducts annual source code reviews through a third-party vendor to identify security vulnerabilities for the repositories related to the X Platform and the Quanta Assist products. The Information Security team raises JIRA tickets for high and critical severity issues, and the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the annual source-code review report for Quanta Assist to determine whether security vulnerabilities for the repositories were identified and documented.

Further, inspected the JIRA tickets for high and critical issues to determine whether the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. 

Additionally, inspected the email communication to determine whether A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual source-code review report for Quanta Assist

controls.title: Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, third-party vendors conduct application level, network level, and API vulnerability assessments and penetration tests for Quanta Assist. Based on the findings of these tests, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team follows up with the relevant stakeholders on appropriate corrective and preventive actions for identified gaps until closure of the gaps. Based on the confirmation received from the relevant stakeholders, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team notifies the third-party vendor to perform rescan to confirm the closure of gaps through email. On completion of rescan, third-party vendor shares the summary report (Compliant Report) with the Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the annual vulnerability assessment and penetration test reports for Quanta Assist to determine whether application level, network level, and API vulnerability and penetration tests for Quanta Assist were conducted, and corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Vulnerability assessment and penetration test (VAPT) reports for all Quanta Assist
Evidence showing that corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: Administrative events, authentication events, and system events generated by CrowdStrike, Microsoft MDM, and firewalls to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents are configured by the Security Operations team to be re-directed to ProACT tool. The SISA MDR team analyses the alerts and generate a Freshdesk ticket for confirmed true positive cases. The Security Operations team analyses these Freshdesk tickets and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within the Freshdesk ticket until closure.

Inspected the configuration settings within ProACT to determine whether alerts generated by CrowdStrike, Microsoft MDM, and firewalls were configured to be re-directed to ProACT.

controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and Data Inventory to determine whether records of personal data collected, purpose of processing, categories of data subjects, third-party vendors safeguards, location and rights available to data subjects. 

Additionally, inspected the document change control log for RoPA and Data inventory to determine whether it was updated and reviewed on an annual basis by Meridian Privacy Team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: RoPA for Quanta Assist.
Data Inventory for Quanta Assist.
JIRA ticket/email communication/documentation showing Data inventory was updated and reviewed on an annual basis by Meridian Privacy Team.
JIRA ticket/email communication/documentation showing Data inventory was updated and reviewed on an annual basis by Meridian Privacy Team.

controls.title: Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and Data Inventory - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Records of processing activities (RoPA) and Data inventories for Meridian website and Workday are prepared and maintained by the Meridian Privacy team identifying the personal data collected, purpose and lawful basis of processing, categories of data subjects, third-party vendors, safeguards, location and rights available to data subjects. The RoPA and Data Inventories are reviewed annually by the Meridian Privacy team.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) and Data Inventories for Meridian website and Workday to determine whether records of personal data collected, purpose and lawful basis of processing, categories of data subjects, third-party vendors safeguards, location and rights available to data subjects were documented and maintained and to determine whether it was reviewed by the Meridian Privacy team on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: RoPA for Meridian website
Evidence showing that RoPA and Data Inventories are reviewed annually by the Meridian Privacy team

controls.title: DPA - Vendors
controls.descriptionText: Vendors are required to sign Data Processing Agreements (DPA), committing to the acceptable use of private and / or confidential data when they have access to user entity data. Agreements are updated when there are system changes that require changes to the customers’ requirements.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of vendors with access to user entity data, inspected the Data Processing Agreements (DPA) to determine whether the vendor had signed the DPA and committed to the acceptable use of private and / or confidential user entity data.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of vendors, Data Processing Agreements (DPA)

controls.title: Consent Management Policy - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for obtaining, documenting and retaining consent. These policies and procedures are approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Consent Management Policy to determine whether policies and procedures exist for obtaining, documenting and retaining consent and to determine whether it was approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Consent Management Policy

controls.title: Consent Tracking - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Explicit consent is obtained from data subjects on Meridian website, Workday, and data centers prior to collection, use or disclosure of their personal information, and details such as identity of data subject, date & time of consent and method of obtaining consent are recorded and maintained within Onetrust tool. Data subjects (candidates, employees, contractors, and visitors) provide their explicit consent by signing the ‘Privacy Notice’ prior to collection, use, and / or disclosure of their personal information. Prior notices and consent records are retained as per Meridian standard retention policies.
controls.testProcedure:
For a sample of consents obtained from data subjects, inspected JIRA ticket to determine whether records of consent was maintained covering the identity of data subject and timestamp of consent taken.

Further, for a sample of consent records, inspected the created/updated date of consent obtained from data subjects to determine whether consent was taken prior to collection, use and disclosure of personal information or when new purpose was identified.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of consents, JIRA ticket showing records of consent was maintained covering the identity of data subject and timestamp of consent taken.
For a sample of consents, JIRA ticket/email communication showing created/updated date of consent obtained from data subjects.

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure - Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user entity access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Data Subject Rights Management Policy to determine whether formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user entity access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Data Subject Rights Management Policy was published on U-Central and was reviewed and approved by CISO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure for managing the user entity access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed.
Screenshot from U-Central showing Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management - Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: Meridian provides user entities with the ability to extract, correct, amend, append, and delete all recorded personal information stored within Quanta Assist. User entities can utilize the features available within Quanta Assist to resolve such data subject requests, or they can reach out to Meridian by sending an email to “privacy@meridian.example”. As a data processor, Meridian forwards all data subject requests to the user entity to verify their authenticity and obtain confirmation to proceed. Meridian has implemented the “product incident management” procedure for receiving, addressing, resolving, and communicating the resolution of these requests from user entities.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Data Subject Rights request Management tracker to determine whether Meridian maintains list of data subject request identifying the type of request received, category of data subject, details of data subject, date received, status, resolution, and response shared by Meridian for fulfilment / denial response sent to data subject in a timely manner.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample data subject requests from user entities, email communication showing Meridian forwards all data subject requests to the user entity to verify their authenticity and obtain confirmation to proceed.
For a sample data subject requests, JIRA tickets showing that the request were addressed and resolved.
For a sample data subject requests, email communication showing that the resolutions were communicated to the user entity.

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: Formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user / employee / applicant access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed. These policies and procedures have been approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Data Subject Rights Management Policy to determine whether formalized policies and procedures exist for managing the user / employee / applicant access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed and to determine whether this policy was approved by Senior Management of Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights Management Policy and Procedure for managing the user / employee / applicant access rights request related to access, erasure, rectification, restrict processing, object and right to be informed

controls.title: Data Subject Rights Management - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: User / employee / applicant can reach out to Meridian by sending an email to “privacy@meridian.example” to resolve data subject requests to extract, correct, amend, append, and / or delete their data. The Meridian Privacy team validates the request and address, resolve, and communicate the resolution of authorized requests.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Data Subject Rights request Management tracker to determine whether  Meridian Privacy team validates the request and address, resolve, and communicate the resolution of authorized requests.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data Subject Rights request Management tracker 'or' email dump of all emails sent to “privacy@meridian.example” for data subject requests from user entities.

For a sample data subject requests from user entities, email communication showing Meridian forwards all data subject requests to the user entity to verify their authenticity and obtain confirmation to proceed.
For a sample data subject requests, JIRA tickets showing that the request were addressed and resolved.
For a sample data subject requests, email communication showing that the resolutions were communicated to the user entity.

controls.title: Breach Management
controls.descriptionText: Meridian creates and retains a complete, accurate, and timely record of detected or reported unauthorized disclosures (including breaches) of Meridian data and user entity data to meet the entity’s objectives related to privacy. Breaches are acted on in accordance with established incident response procedures. Meridian provides notification of data breaches and incidents to the affected user entities, regulators, and others, as applicable.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of data privacy incidents, inspected the breach register to determine whether the breach was acted on in accordance with the established incident response procedures, and whether the affected user entities, regulators, and others, as applicable were notified by the Meridian Privacy Team.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of breaches, JIRA ticket/email communication breaches are resolved.
For a sample of breaches, email communication showing Meridian provides notification of data breaches and incidents to the affected user entities, regulators, and others, as applicable.

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) - Quanta Assist & Quanta Assist
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Meridian Privacy Team performs Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for Quanta Assist to evaluate and mitigate privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the DPIA reports for Quanta Assist to determine whether Meridian's Privacy Team evaluated and mitigated privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DPIA report for Quanta Assist
Annual DPIA report for Quanta Self-Serve
Annual DPIA report for Quanta Capture NG
Annual DPIA report for Quanta Assist
Annual DPIA report for Quanta Discover
Annual DPIA report for Quanta Analyze
Annual DPIA report for Zephyr Console

controls.title: Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Meridian Privacy Team performs Data Protection Impact Assessment (DPIA) for the Meridian Website and Workday to evaluate and mitigate privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the DPIA reports for Meridian Website and Workday to determine whether Meridian's Privacy Team evaluated and mitigated privacy risks related to the nature, scope, context, purpose, necessity, and proportionality of personal information; data life cycle management; data subject rights; and sub-processors on an annual basis.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DPIA report for Meridian Website and Workday.

controls.title: Cookie Notice - Data Controller
controls.descriptionText:
Formalized Cookie Notice for managing information collected through cookies is developed and published on Meridian Website and Workday covering the type of cookies collected, purpose, lawful ground of processing, and changes to Cookie Notice. It is approved by the Senior Management of Meridian. These notices communicate the following to the Data Subjects:
•	Purpose for collecting personal information and types
•	Choice, consent, and security for privacy
•	Methods of collection, use, access, retention, and disposal 
•	Disclosure to third parties 
•	Quality, including data subjects’ responsibilities for quality 
•	Monitoring and enforcement
•	Consequences of denying or withdrawing consent and / or data transfers
controls.testProcedure: Inspected Meridian website URL (Cookie Notice | Meridian) to determine whether Meridian cookie notice was available on Meridian website and addressed matters related to the type of cookies collected, purpose, lawful ground of processing, and changes to cookie notice.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian Cookie Notice available for Meridian's website.
Cookie Notice available on Workday.
